app.name: ChatPDF
app.bestFor: Fast, no-friction chat with a single PDF for quick one-off questions and summaries.
app.rankBasis: The most consistently cited dedicated PDF-chat tool across the three 2026 comparison sources in this file, with a large consumer footprint but limited verified enterprise review volume.
app.pricingFree: Free plan with daily document and question limits
app.pricingPaid: Plus plan available; current official page does not expose a stable public price and allowance in static content
pros: very low-friction upload-and-chat workflow
answers link back to source pages
reviewers find it effective for quick one-document research
cons: free usage is tightly limited
less capable than research workspaces for cross-document synthesis
scanned or complex-layout PDFs can reduce answer quality

app.name: Adobe Acrobat AI Assistant
app.pricing: Paid (AI Assistant add-on ~$5/mo on top of Acrobat)
app.bestFor: Editing and summarizing existing PDFs with page-level citations plus enterprise security and redaction.
app.rankBasis: Backed by Acrobat's category-leading installed base and thousands of G2 reviews; 2026 PDF-AI roundups repeatedly rank it near the top for combining AI with full PDF editing.
app.pricingFree: No standing full AI Assistant plan; limited trial prompts may be offered
app.pricingPaid: AI Assistant is sold with qualifying Acrobat plans; current price varies by Acrobat plan and region
pros: AI works inside a mature full-featured PDF editor
page-level citations make answers easier to verify
strong enterprise security and document controls
cons: AI access is layered onto an already complex Acrobat plan structure
desktop software can feel heavy and crowded
reviewers frequently criticize subscription cost and upselling

app.name: Google NotebookLM
app.bestFor: Best free option; retrieves source text before answering, so it resists hallucinated citations.
app.rankBasis: The leading free research-workspace choice across 2026 PDF-chat comparisons, supported by Google's broad adoption and strong community enthusiasm for grounded citations and Audio Overviews.
app.pricingFree: Free — up to 100 notebooks, 50 sources/notebook, 50 chat queries/day and 3 audio generations/day
app.pricingPaid: Higher limits included with eligible Google AI and Workspace plans; price depends on the parent subscription
features: Study guides, mind maps and reports
pros: answers are tightly grounded in selected source material
excellent multi-document synthesis at no cost
distinctive Audio Overviews make long material accessible
cons: not a PDF editor or form-filling tool
output quality depends on source parsing and notebook organization
higher limits are bundled into broader Google subscriptions

app.name: AskYourPDF
app.bestFor: Free multi-document chat when you need to query across several PDFs at once.
app.rankBasis: Appears in multiple 2026 PDF-chat roundups and claims more than five million users, but has a smaller independent review base than ChatPDF, Adobe or Google.
app.pricingFree: Free — 1 document/day, 100 pages/document, 15 MB uploads, 50 questions/day and 3 conversations/day
app.pricingPaid: Premium $11.99/mo billed annually — 50 documents/day, 2,500 pages/document and 1,200 questions/day
pros: generous question allowance on the free tier
supports querying multiple documents together
wide model choice on paid tiers
cons: advanced models consume separate credits
free tier permits only one document upload per day
interface and plan limits are more complex than simpler rivals

app.name: Humata AI
app.bestFor: Research-oriented Q&A over long technical and academic PDFs with cited answers.
app.rankBasis: A recurring research-oriented recommendation in PDF-chat roundups, though its independent review footprint and community mention volume remain modest.
app.pricingFree: Free — basic features for up to 60 pages/mo
app.pricingPaid: Expert $9.99/mo — 500 pages/mo for up to 3 users; additional pages $0.02 each
pros: focused interface for technical and academic reading
answers include document references
entry plan supports a small group rather than only one user
cons: usage is metered by processed pages
OCR and stronger controls are reserved for higher tiers
less useful for editing or visually manipulating PDFs

app.name: PDFgear
app.bestFor: Genuinely free editor with an AI copilot; the budget pick for students and small teams.
app.rankBasis: A prominent free-editor recommendation in 2026 roundups; PDFgear reports a 4.9 rating from 10,000+ reviews, although those reviews are not from one independently audited marketplace.
app.pricingFree: Free — desktop, mobile and online PDF editing, conversion and AI tools
pros: unusually complete editor without a subscription
clean desktop experience for common PDF tasks
reviewers value the breadth of free conversion and editing tools
cons: AI and online features can still require uploading document content
advanced enterprise administration is absent
some complex editing and OCR cases trail Acrobat-grade tools

app.name: Smallpdf
app.bestFor: All-in-one web PDF suite (convert, compress, sign) with AI summarize and chat layered on.
app.rankBasis: A mature, widely reviewed PDF utility with thousands of G2 and Trustpilot reviews; 2026 AI-PDF roundups include it mainly for its broad tool suite rather than best-in-class chat.
app.pricingFree: Free plan — limited daily access to core web tools
app.pricingPaid: Pro subscription available; official price is localized and varies by billing region
pros: very broad set of everyday PDF utilities
simple web workflow with no installation required
large user base and polished cross-platform apps
cons: free users face task and feature limits
subscriptions can be expensive for occasional use
AI analysis is less research-focused than dedicated PDF-chat products

skill.name: Stirling-PDF
skill.bestFor: The de-facto open-source PDF toolkit: 50+ local operations — merge, split, OCR, convert, sign, redact, compress — fully self-hosted. The recognized OSS PDF leader (~88k stars).
skill.rankBasis: The recognized all-in-one open-source PDF leader at about 87.9k GitHub stars, with active daily development and unusually broad self-hosting community adoption.
pros: keeps sensitive documents on infrastructure you control
replaces dozens of single-purpose PDF web tools
large active community and frequent releases
cons: self-hosters own upgrades, storage and security
large feature surface can make the UI feel busy
resource-heavy OCR and conversion jobs require capable hardware

skill.name: MarkItDown (Microsoft)
skill.bestFor: Microsoft's document-to-Markdown converter (PDF, Office, images, audio) built to feed clean text to LLMs; the highest-starred tool in this space (~169k stars).
skill.rankBasis: The highest-starred document-conversion project in this category at about 168.7k GitHub stars, with Microsoft stewardship and broad LLM-pipeline adoption.
features: CLI, Python API and plugin support
pros: simple output format designed for LLM consumption
handles many common document types behind one API
lightweight integration and strong project visibility
cons: prioritizes text conversion over faithful layout reconstruction
complex tables and multi-column PDFs can lose structure
not an editor, viewer or full document-management system

skill.name: MinerU (OpenDataLab)
skill.bestFor: High-accuracy PDF-to-Markdown/JSON extraction; the standout for complex academic papers and CJK layouts (~76k stars).
skill.rankBasis: A leading complex-document parser at about 75.6k GitHub stars, repeatedly highlighted in PDF-to-Markdown comparisons for academic, formula and CJK layouts.
features: OCR for scanned and multilingual documents
pros: strong results on dense academic and technical layouts
good handling of formulas, tables and CJK text
produces assets and structure useful for RAG pipelines
cons: model downloads and GPU acceleration add operational weight
installation is more involved than lightweight parsers
results still need review on unusual scans and layouts

skill.name: Docling (IBM / DS4SD)
skill.bestFor: IBM's document parser (PDF, DOCX, PPTX) with strong layout and table understanding; a default in the LlamaIndex/LangChain ecosystem (~64k stars).
skill.rankBasis: A fast-growing IBM-originated parser at about 63.7k GitHub stars, with first-class integrations across major RAG frameworks and frequent 2026 comparison coverage.
pros: strong structured output for retrieval pipelines
supports more than PDFs in one toolkit
active ecosystem integrations and enterprise-grade provenance
cons: large dependency stack compared with basic PDF libraries
accurate complex parsing can be compute-intensive
not intended for interactive PDF editing

skill.name: Anthropic pdf skill (official)
skill.bestFor: The official Anthropic SKILL.md that powers Claude's PDF work — form filling, text/table extraction, merge, and generation. The reference Agent Skill for PDFs (in anthropics/skills, ~164k stars).
skill.rankBasis: The official Anthropic reference PDF skill inside the roughly 163.9k-star anthropics/skills repository; the parent-star count signals visibility, not isolated skill usage.
pros: official, inspectable guidance for reliable agent PDF work
covers creation, editing and QA rather than one narrow operation
uses established PDF libraries and render verification
cons: requires a compatible agent runtime and local dependencies
repository stars cannot be attributed to this skill alone
complex visual edits still need iterative rendering and inspection

skill.name: marker (Datalab)
skill.bestFor: Fast, high-quality PDF/EPUB/DOCX to Markdown conversion with tables, math, and images; a general-purpose favorite (~38k stars).
skill.rankBasis: A popular conversion-focused project at about 37.8k GitHub stars, regularly included in 2026 PDF-to-Markdown comparisons for its accuracy and broad format support.
pros: high-quality structured output across varied documents
good table, math and image handling
usable locally with configurable models
cons: high-quality conversion can require substantial RAM or GPU resources
some advanced modes depend on external model services
not a general-purpose PDF editor

skill.name: pdfplumber
skill.bestFor: Precise text, table, and layout extraction from born-digital PDFs; the go-to Python library for table scraping (~11k stars).
skill.rankBasis: A long-standing Python extraction staple at about 10.6k GitHub stars, widely cited for inspectable character-level layout and table extraction.
pros: fine-grained control over PDF layout primitives
excellent debugging tools for table extraction
pure Python workflow familiar to data teams
cons: works best on machine-generated rather than scanned PDFs
table settings often need document-specific tuning
does not provide OCR or high-level AI understanding by itself

skill.name: PyMuPDF
skill.bestFor: Fast low-level PDF/XPS parsing, rendering, and editing (MuPDF bindings); the performance workhorse under many pipelines (~10k stars).
skill.rankBasis: A performance-oriented PDF foundation at about 10.3k GitHub stars, broadly used in production pipelines for fast rendering, extraction and manipulation.
skill.pricingPaid: Commercial licensing available from Artifex for proprietary distribution
features: Search, OCR hooks and format conversion
pros: very fast and memory-efficient core
broad low-level manipulation API
handles rendering and editing as well as extraction
cons: AGPL obligations can conflict with closed-source distribution
low-level API requires more engineering than turnkey converters
documentation and coordinate systems have a learning curve

skill.name: pypdf
skill.bestFor: Pure-Python library to split, merge, crop, and extract PDF pages and metadata; a lightweight, dependency-free staple (~10k stars).
skill.rankBasis: A widely used pure-Python PDF library at about 10.1k GitHub stars, valued for lightweight page manipulation and an active maintenance cadence.
pros: pure Python with a small dependency footprint
straightforward API for common structural operations
permissive license and active community
cons: no built-in rendering or OCR engine
text extraction struggles with visually complex layouts
large or malformed PDFs can be slow and memory-intensive

skill.name: claude-office-skills (tfriedel)
skill.bestFor: Community office-document Agent Skills pack (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX) with automation helpers for Claude Code (~800 stars).
skill.rankBasis: A niche community office-skill pack with about 796 GitHub stars; useful cross-format coverage, but little independent review or usage evidence for its PDF workflow specifically.
features: DOCX, PPTX and XLSX companion skills
pros: covers an entire office-document workflow in one pack
plain-text skills are easy to inspect and adapt
useful starting automation for repeated document tasks
cons: small independent community signal
quality depends on local tools and the agent runtime
parent repository adoption does not validate each document skill

notes: App list draws on multiple independent test/comparison roundups; ChatPDF, Acrobat AI Assistant, and NotebookLM top several lists. Reviewers flag confident-wrong answers with plausible citations as the main failure mode, favoring retrieval-first tools (NotebookLM). Skill/Repo track is now ranked by real quality, reputation, and adoption (GitHub stars) rather than form factor — kind ('skill' vs 'repo') is only a tag. It mixes notable open-source PDF repos with genuine Agent Skills: Stirling-PDF is the recognized de-facto OSS PDF toolkit, followed by the big document-extraction repos (MarkItDown, MinerU, Docling, marker) and Anthropic's official pdf skill, with pdfplumber/PyMuPDF/pypdf as lower-level libraries and community skill packs in the tail. Star counts verified via the GitHub API (Jul 2026).
